Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»Paris 2024 Olympics: What time does Akani Simbine run?

It’s Simbine’s time.

Team SA has three representatives in the 100m semi-finals, a blue-chip event at the Paris 2024 Olympics. Akani Simbine hopes to improve on his fourth place in Tokyo three years ago.

South African sprint sensation Akani Simbine ran a time of 9.93 seconds at Tokyo 2020 in the 100m final. That meant he missed a bronze medal by a heartbreaking four-hundredths of a second behind Canada’s Andre De Grasse. Italy’s Lamont Marcell Jacobs was the surprise gold medalist four years ago in a wide-open field that Simbine fancied his chances of winning in the Land of the Rising Sun. At Rio 2016; medalists in the 100m event were iconic Jamaican Usain Bolt with gold, USA’s Justin Gatlin claiming silver and De Grasse scooping the bronze. Rio and Tokyo contain painful memories for Akani Simbine, but Paris 2024 can change all of that.

30-year-old Simbine is a major candidate to claim a medal in Paris. However, there are dangerous contenders everywhere you look including Jamaican Oblique Seville and reigning Olympic champion Lamont Marcell Jacobs from Italy. There’s a strong African presence inSimbine’s semi-final going at 20:14. Alongside him is Liberia’s Emmanuel Matadi, Nigeria’s Kayinsola Ajayi and Letsile Tebogo from Botswana. Simbine is the senior man out of Team SA’s three candidates in the semi-finals. The others are 20-year-old Benjamin Richardson and 23-year-old Shaun Maswanganyi.

It was always going to be difficult to live up to Usain Bolt’s ridiculous legacy. The great man crossed the line first in eight Olympic sprint finals, including an unprecedented triple-double in the 100m and 200m events across three consecutive games in Beijing, London and Rio. Will we ever see someone like him again? Three Jamaicans are in the running for the title of world’s fastest man and Olympic champion in 2024; Oblique Seville, Ackeem Blake and Kishane Thompson.

Let us know by clicking on the comment tab below this article or emailinginfo@thesouthafrican.com. Meanwhile, you can also send a WhatsApp to 060 011 021 1. Follow@TheSAnews on XandThe South African on Facebookfor your social fix.

title: Paris 2024 Olympics: What time does Akani Simbine run?
author: James Freemantle
description: The men's 100m sprint is a highlight of the Paris 2024 Olympics. Akani Simbine hopes to improve on his fourth place in Tokyo three years ago.
